positional_bias.notes: max_share 0.33 < 0.40 threshold; all four positions occupied

q1.original: forces the model to produce intermediate steps that become part of its own context for generating the next step
q1.fixed: gives the model's own earlier steps as context for each next step

q2.original: Few-shot CoT, because providing example reasoning triples shows the model the exact style and structure expected
q2.fixed: Few-shot CoT, because reasoning triples show the exact style expected

q3.original: A user message that ends with "Reason step by step before giving your final answer." and contains no worked examples
q3.fixed: A message with a step-by-step trigger phrase and no worked examples

q6.original: Place the step-by-step instruction in the system parameter of client.messages.create() so it applies to all messages
q6.fixed: Place the instruction in the system parameter so it applies to every message

soft_findings: q1 distractor 'instructs the model to search the internet for supporting evidence' is borderline implausible (score=2); beginner may not know LLMs cannot browse, but the feedback corrects this clearly
distractor_plausibility.notes: absurd_rate 0.042 < 0.25 threshold; all other distractors represent plausible beginner misconceptions

objective_coverage.notes: all 6 LOs covered one-to-one; q7 short-answer covers recall of reasoning-triple parts (supplementary)

beginner_scope.notes: CoT expanded on first use in q1 stem; all stems use plain language; no out-of-scope concepts; distractors draw only on 13.1–13.3 prior knowledge; q6 API usage matches §5.3 and §6 exactly

verdict: PASS — all 5 checks pass after auto-fix of length bias in q1, q2, q3, q6; quiz.gift and quiz.meta.json updated
